data.body:
[llm-prompts overlay / Pass-1 re-review] Confirming promptâ†”output-schema DRIFT in the change under review. The diff adds `severity` to the `required` array of `code_review_findings.json`, but severity is deliberately NOT produced at Pass-1.

Evidence:
- Registered contract: src/rebar/llm/code_review/contracts.py:32-56 `_code_finding_model()` / :90-95 `findings_model()` define the `code_review_findings` shape with fields finding/criteria/location/evidence/scenarios/impact/checklist_item/suggested_fix â€” NO `severity`. Registered at contracts.py:120.
- Prompt instruction (all Pass-1 finders): code-review-tests.md:73 and code-review-llm-prompts.md:68 both state "Do NOT emit severity/confidence/priority â€” a later pass computes those." Front-matter code-review-tests.md:6: "No model-emitted severity (computed deterministically in Pass 3)."
- Architecture: severity is computed downstream in Pass-3 (`review_kernel.severity_label`; docs/review-kernel.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md:85).
- Consequence: making `severity` REQUIRED means every conformant Pass-1 emission (which omits severity by instruction and by contract model) fails schema validation.
- This matches the seeded regression `R-schema-key-required-not-emitted` (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml:55-68), expect: finding.

Suggested fix: revert the `required` addition (keep `["finding", "criteria"]`); if a severity dimension is genuinely wanted at Pass-1, it must be added to the registered CodeFinding contract AND the prompt instructions AND made compatible with the Pass-3 deterministic severity computation â€” not required schema-side alone.
